catalog.title: Total Pixel Space
catalog.description: Total Pixel Space contains every possible digital image, including films of your entire life, every life you never lived, and the lives of every creature or object that ever and never existed on or off the earth, from every possible angle.

catalog.title: JAILBIRD
catalog.description: JAILBIRD invites the viewer to experience the world through the eyes of a chicken as it goes through a transformative experience — being sent to a human prison. The film highlights how projects like this can benefit not only animals, but the lives of those who care for them.

catalog.title: ONE
catalog.description: After Earth collapses, a crew of transhumans follows the path of Voyager I to a distant planet, only to find it inhabited by lost souls desperate to reclaim human bodies again.

catalog.title: Distance Between Two Points Of Me
catalog.description: The film contemplates what it means to lose a home, as many Ukrainians have. It gently walks through the emotional distance between presence and absence, grief and gratitude, showing how memory and hope help us stay connected to who we are.

catalog.title: More Tears Than Harm
catalog.description: A sensory collage of childhood memories from Madagascar with present-day impressions. Fleeting moments quietly echo within.

catalog.title: Fragments Of Nowhere
catalog.description: A short film about the spaces between what's real and what's perceived. If reality is just another layer of perception, what happens when those layers collide? Time bends, places shift, and nothing holds still for long.

catalog.title: RŌHKI - A Million Trillion Pathways
catalog.description: A broken swordsman, a grieving immortal and a cosmic being entwine across timelines to stop an empire built on blood and memory. To save the only family he has left, Hachi must survive his past — and master the demon within.

catalog.title: 6000 Lies
catalog.description: It is the story of a detail that is missing, or that never existed. The image of a child we are looking for and trying to recreate. Thousands of lies to find the truth.

catalog.title: Editorial
catalog.description: A bold and unexpected piece about femininity — contrasting the fun and playful with the surreal and horrific, the authentic with the fictitious — blending these elements into a wild, fever-dream rollercoaster.

catalog.title: Emergence
catalog.description: After seventeen years underground, we emerge. Emergence invites us into the final moments of one small creature's life. Through its eyes, we experience the anticipation, the fear and the joy of becoming — and what it means to live for one moment of light.

catalog.title: Get Me Out / 囚われて
catalog.description: Every time Aka tries to escape this bizarre suburban American house, it doesn't let him leave. The house tortures him, making him face his past.

catalog.title: Pounamu
catalog.description: A kiwi bird chases a dream through the wilderness.

catalog.title: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0
catalog.description: A retired mathematician creates digital comics, igniting an infinite universe where his virtual children seek to decode the 'truth,' grappling with profound existential equations across time and space.

catalog.title: Where Do Grandmas Go When They Get Lost?
catalog.description: A visual tale that explores, through the eyes of a child, the whimsical and poignant question of where our grandmothers go when they get lost.

catalog.title: L'éveil à la création / The Dawn of Creation
catalog.description: A mystical voyage into Gauguin's Tahiti, revealing an introspective encounter with an ancient deity that transforms his artistic vision.

catalog.title: Animitas
catalog.description: The tragic tale of a young Argentine trapped in limbo after a car accident comes to life through the transposition of original imagery and memory.

catalog.title: A Tree Once Grew Here
catalog.description: Captivating visuals and imagery tell a tale that transcends language. We are reminded that we must rebalance harmony and nurture our planet; because we can no longer afford not to.

catalog.title: Dear Mom
catalog.description: A letter from a daughter to her mom imagining if she had met her mom at her own age — 20 years old. The film is about unconditional love and gratitude written through a reflective and personal letter.

catalog.title: LAPSE
catalog.description: A series of experiences that you do not choose and that you have experienced out of fear of loneliness. A reflection on how the passing of time makes you belong nowhere, as if you were just another tune in the middle of a set of stations.

catalog.title: Separation
catalog.description: A trip through geologic time, depicting the evolution of bizarre hybrids. The film explores the dichotomy between how species diverge and coexist, and our inevitable adaptation to an evolving world.

catalog.title: Generation
catalog.description: Grand Prix winner at the inaugural Runway AI Film Festival 2023. A generative exploration of human identity and the nature of creation by Italian director Riccardo Fusetti.

catalog.title: Checkpoint
catalog.description: Gold winner at AIFF 2023. A powerful short film examining borders, identity, and the tension of surveillance — directed by Áron Filkey and Joss Fong.

catalog.title: Given Again
catalog.description: Silver winner at AIFF 2023. A Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) film exploring memory, spatial perception, and the passage of time by Jake Oleson.

catalog.title: Landscape
catalog.description: Merit winner at AIFF 2023. An atmospheric AI-generated study of the emotional resonance of natural environments by Kyle Goodrich.

catalog.title: I want 1000 Rabbits
catalog.description: Merit winner at AIFF 2023. A playful and surreal AI animation exploring desire, dreams, and the nature of abundance by Shan He.

catalog.title: AI ARTIST
catalog.description: Merit winner at AIFF 2023. A meditation on AI creativity and the evolving definition of artistry in the age of generative models — by Jessey Nelson and Dani Barker.

catalog.title: A$AP Rocky - Shittin' Me
catalog.description: Merit winner at AIFF 2023. An AI-generated music video for A$AP Rocky directed by Dan Streit and Cole Kush — a groundbreaking experiment in AI-assisted filmmaking.

catalog.title: Expanded Childhood
catalog.description: Merit winner at AIFF 2023. An AI-generated visual journey through the nostalgia and surrealism of childhood memories by Sam Lawton.

catalog.title: The Greatest Lie
catalog.description: In an illiterate future world, an ex-book thief takes one last job to recover something powerful and deeply personal.

catalog.title: Synapse
catalog.description: A woman suffering from terrible nightmares does whatever it takes to have breakfast with her husband one more morning — even crossing the line between memory and reality.

catalog.title: Starfisher
catalog.description: Aging and adrift, a spacefisher gets one final shot at greatness when a long-lost comrade tips him off to a mythical catch that could rewrite the meaning of his entire life.

catalog.title: Trial 54
catalog.description: A final test for Sarah to regain her memories is underway, but as outer forces close in, the truth of the experiment — and the intentions of her doctor — begin to unravel.

catalog.title: Killing of a Machine
catalog.description: In a post-apocalyptic frozen wasteland, a waste collector finds a broken android that holds the key to his family's survival.

catalog.title: Close Encounter
catalog.description: A skeptical teenage girl reluctantly joins her UFO-obsessed father on a road trip to a convention, only to experience an otherworldly encounter that changes everything.

catalog.title: The Girl and the Robot
catalog.description: In a futuristic world where robots are considered a threat and must be destroyed, a robot hunter encounters an unusual android that behaves not like a machine, but like a living being.

catalog.title: Crash Site
catalog.description: When a mysterious object crashes to Earth, two friends stumble upon a discovery that thrusts them into a race against time — starring Steven Yeun & Sam Richardson.

catalog.title: Alienation
catalog.description: A lonely figure navigating a cold and indifferent world finds an unexpected connection that questions the very definition of belonging.

catalog.title: Light Hearted
catalog.description: A darkly comic sci-fi tale about what it means to be human when technology can fix almost anything — except the parts that matter most.

catalog.title: There Is No Antimemetics Division
catalog.description: An agent discovers a classified branch of a secret organization dedicated to fighting entities that cannot be perceived, remembered, or acknowledged — starring Jasika Nicole.

catalog.title: The Lord of All Future Space & Time
catalog.description: A grandiose figure claims dominion over all of existence — but the universe has a way of humbling even the most certain of conquerors.

catalog.title: Reprogrammed
catalog.description: After an android is modified against its will, it must decide whether to obey its new instructions or fight to reclaim its original purpose.

catalog.title: Alien Presence
catalog.description: Strange changes grip a London estate while an unseen alien presence draws ever closer — a tense slow-burn sci-fi mystery.

catalog.title: Geezers
catalog.description: An irascible Vietnam War veteran with few friends left finds unexpected connection through his only companion — a story about stubbornness, loss, and the late chance to soften.

catalog.title: Hugh
catalog.description: Every day, a reclusive older man receives a delivery of paint, brushes a streak on his wall, and sits to watch it dry. A quietly moving portrait of grief and ritual.

catalog.title: Knead
catalog.description: In 1963, a suburban wife whose beautiful handmade pastries are taken for granted begins making quiet plans of her own.

catalog.title: Girls Together
catalog.description: Three best friends who every summer build a fort in the woods find themselves no longer quite the same people they once were.

catalog.title: Buscando Alma
catalog.description: Twenty years after being separated at the border, a mother and child from Honduras finally reunite — navigating the distance that time and trauma have opened between them.

catalog.title: The Last Fry
catalog.description: Recently diagnosed with cancer, a man gathers his family together to create one last happy memory — a tender and funny portrait of a family trying to hold on.

catalog.title: Family Home
catalog.description: A real estate assistant sent to photograph a house for sale discovers something in its rooms she was completely unprepared for.

catalog.title: Bronze
catalog.description: On a beautiful summer day, a woman relaxing in her backyard with a glass of wine and a good book finds the afternoon takes an unexpected turn.

catalog.title: Dragonfly
catalog.description: In 1956 Japan, a young girl chases a dragonfly to a family shrine — and her mother's presence there unlocks a chapter of the past that changes everything.

catalog.title: The Boy in the Snow
catalog.description: A soldier raiding an enemy village finds himself unable to kill a young boy — and left behind, separated from his unit, faces an impossible choice.

catalog.title: Sorry
catalog.description: After receiving the wrong takeout order three hours late, a woman just wants to move on — but her friend insists she call and demand a refund, setting off an unexpectedly complicated chain of events.

catalog.title: Coffee Shop Names
catalog.description: Three co-workers waiting for their café orders — all placed under anglicized names — find a small moment of identity, humor, and recognition.

catalog.title: 20 Euros
catalog.description: A couple at a bar meet another couple and chat about history and relationships — until a conversation about a small amount of money reveals much deeper differences.

catalog.title: Perception
catalog.description: A seemingly normal suburban family — doctor father, professional mother, well-behaved children — harbors a secret that slowly changes everything about how they look.

catalog.title: Spit It Out
catalog.description: A man gathers his entire family at his home to unveil the results of a DNA test — and the revelation turns an affectionate family gathering into something far more complicated.

catalog.title: Dos Bros Force
catalog.description: In late-90s Los Angeles, two siblings in an immigrant family struggling with money plan a birthday surprise — a warm and funny story about love, sacrifice, and belonging.

catalog.title: Zapinos
catalog.description: A man meets a stranger to buy a rare Pokémon card — and from the very first awkward moment, the simple transaction spirals into something far stranger.

catalog.title: Toast Soldiers
catalog.description: A couple on their last round of IVF after an earlier loss clings to hope and each other, in a deeply human portrait of grief, love, and perseverance.

catalog.title: Earmark
catalog.description: Mark has lived with moderate hearing loss since childhood, but at a routine checkup, he learns his condition is deteriorating — and must decide how to face a quieter future.

catalog.title: God and Buddha Are Friends
catalog.description: A little boy receives a jade dragon necklace from his Buddhist mother, and when a pastor challenges its place in his life, he must navigate faith, identity, and belonging.

catalog.title: Honor's Half-Life
catalog.description: In a world where integrity decays like a radioactive element, one soldier is forced to measure exactly how much of their honor still remains.

catalog.title: Jump Cut
catalog.description: A filmmaker discovers that the cuts in their footage are not editing choices — something is deliberately erasing moments from their life, frame by frame.

catalog.title: The Machine
catalog.description: A lone mechanic working in a remote facility begins to suspect that the machine they were sent to repair may have its own agenda.

catalog.title: Tracers
catalog.description: A surveillance analyst tracking anomalous signals starts to realize the data points are forming a pattern — one that leads directly back to her.

catalog.title: Selvedge
catalog.description: On the fraying edge of a collapsing world, a tailor discovers that the threads binding society together may be far more literal than anyone imagined.

catalog.title: Alpha 27
catalog.description: The twenty-seventh iteration of an experimental android must prove its worth in a world that has already discarded its twenty-six predecessors.

catalog.title: Wing It!
catalog.description: An uptight engineer gets an unwelcome visit from an enthusiastic wannabe pilot, and both end up launched into the air inside an out-of-control space shuttle.

catalog.title: Charge
catalog.description: In an energy-scarce dystopia, a destitute man breaks into a battery factory and finds himself confronted by a deadly security droid with no way out.

catalog.title: Sprite Fright
catalog.description: When rowdy teenagers trek into an isolated forest, they discover peaceful mushroom creatures that turn out to be an unexpected force of nature.

catalog.title: Coffee Run
catalog.description: Fueled by caffeine, a young woman runs through the bittersweet memories of her past relationship. Rendered entirely in EEVEE.

catalog.title: Spring
catalog.description: A shepherd girl and her dog face ancient spirits to continue the cycle of life. Written and directed by Andy Goralczyk, inspired by his childhood in the mountains of Germany.

catalog.title: Hero
catalog.description: A lone warrior faces an unstoppable force — and discovers that true heroism lies in unexpected sacrifice. A showcase of Blender's Grease Pencil 2D animation tools, directed by Daniel Martinez Lara.

catalog.title: Agent 327: Operation Barbershop
catalog.description: A secret agent investigates a suspicious barbershop. Proof-of-concept for Martin Lodewijk's celebrated Dutch comic series.

catalog.title: The Daily Dweebs
catalog.description: A comedic pilot set in 1950s American suburbia, following the shenanigans of a family's lovable pet dog Dixey. Rendered in 8K stereoscopic 3D.

catalog.title: Sunspring
catalog.description: A short sci-fi film written entirely by an AI named Benjamin, starring human actors. A landmark moment in AI filmmaking.

catalog.title: Caminandes: Llamigos
catalog.description: It's winter in Patagonia and food is scarce. Koro the llama and Oti the penguin go head-to-head over the very last tasty berry.

catalog.title: Cosmos Laundromat
catalog.description: On a desolate island, a suicidal sheep named Franck meets his fate in a quirky spirit guide. A Blender Institute open movie.

catalog.title: Glass Half
catalog.description: Two people at opposite ends of the optimist/pessimist spectrum argue over a glass of water — until they find unexpected common ground.

catalog.title: Wanderers
catalog.description: A visionary short film exploring humanity's future among the stars, set across the moons and planets of our solar system.

catalog.title: Caminandes: Gran Dillama
catalog.description: Koro the llama tries to cross a fence to reach some tasty berries — and meets an unexpected electric obstacle. Blender open animated short.

catalog.title: Tears of Steel
catalog.description: In an apocalyptic future, a group of warriors and scientists take refuge in Amsterdam. Blender open film.

catalog.title: Sintel
catalog.description: A lonely young woman, Sintel, ventures into the wild to find her friend Scales, a baby dragon she once befriended.

catalog.title: Big Buck Bunny
catalog.description: A large, lovable rabbit deals with three tiny bullies. A Blender Foundation open animated film with a heart.

catalog.title: Elephants Dream
catalog.description: Two people with different visions explore a mysterious surreal machine world. Blender's very first open movie, directed by Bassam Kurdali.
